MicroLearn/UW IT Capstone, Winter 2026

Turn ad space into brain space.

What if you could use ads to influence yourself? MicroLearn is a Chrome extension that swaps ad slots for short learning cards while you browse.

Role: Product Manager & Developer Team of 5 Manifest V3 Claude API

One ad slot, cycling real lessons

The demo

Watch it work.

A short walkthrough: set up a learning path, turn the extension on over a live page, and watch the ad slots fill with lessons.

The problem

Attention is already spent. It just goes the wrong way.

Ads shape behavior through repeated exposure. A person can meet thousands of ad messages in a day. Each one is built to land in two or three seconds. Learning works the other way. It asks for time, focus, and a separate study block. Most people cannot find that time.

$1T+
spent on advertising worldwide each year, all competing for attention
Global ad spend, industry estimates
37%
of students reported moderate to severe depressive symptoms
Healthy Minds Study, 2024–25
~39%
of students with symptoms did not receive counseling last year
Healthy Minds Study, 2024–25

The idea

Do not fight for new attention. Reclaim the attention people already give to ads.

The user picks a direction, such as a certification or a wellbeing habit. Routine browsing becomes learning time. There is no new app and no schedule change. The build pairs two proven ideas: spaced repetition for memory, and advertising's passive influence through repeated exposure.

Do not waste attention. Transform it.
MicroLearn guiding principle

How it works

Five steps, run on every page.

A content script watches the page. When it finds an ad slot, it hands the space to a learning card and renders in under two seconds.

01

Detect

Scan the page for ad slots using selectors, size patterns, and community filter lists.

02

Replace

Hide the ad and drop a learning card in its place without breaking the layout.

03

Read

Pull the user's mode, path, and subjects from Chrome Storage.

04

Generate

Call the Claude API with the topic and a rotating style hint that keeps output varied.

05

Render

Parse the reply into a headline and tagline and show it inside the card.

In the wild

Real ad slots, on live sites.

The team validated detection across ten major sites. Here the extension runs on a news homepage and a speed-test page, with learning cards sitting where the ads were.

CNN, educational mode. CCNA cards fill the top banner and a sidebar slot, each sized to the ad it replaced.
Speedtest, educational mode. CCNA cards take the side and banner slots while the page works as normal.

The product

One page sets everything.

The user picks a content mode, a learning path, and up to five subjects, or builds a custom group that mixes subjects from any path. A dashboard tracks lessons viewed by day, week, and month.

Preferences and dashboard. Educational mode, CCNA path.
Custom groups. Pull subjects from any path in the current mode.

Two modes

One format. Two opposite jobs.

Educational

Teach a fact.

Security+ and CCNA material in a dense, glanceable card.

Content rotates. The same fact never shows twice.
Behavioral

Shift a mindset.

Stress, resilience, and money habits, in direct second person.

Content repeats. Repetition is the mechanism.
Custom

In either mode, pick a preset path or go custom and add any subject you want. The engine writes cards for whatever you choose.

The card format: every lesson is two lines

HEADLINE: 5 to 8 words // the core fact or statement
TAGLINE:  8 to 15 words // why it matters or reinforcement
// under 25 words total. Built to read like a billboard, not a textbook.

Under the hood

Client-side. No backend.

Three parts carry the system, and Chrome Storage holds preferences and a pool of thirty pre-generated lessons, so cards appear from local storage instead of a live call each time.

content script

Find and replace

Scans the DOM, reaches ad slots buried in wrappers, sizes a card to the original, and keeps the page layout intact.

service worker

Build and batch

Constructs prompts, calls the API in parallel groups, stores the pool, removes repeats, and regenerates when it runs out.

popup + preferences

Control

The toggle turns replacement on or off. The preferences page handles onboarding, modes, subjects, theme, and the key.

chrome storage + claude api

Store and generate

Preferences and the lesson pool stay on the device. The only outbound call carries the topic and prompt, not identity.

My role

What I built.

A five-person team built MicroLearn over eight weeks with rotating project management. My work centered on reaching and detecting ad slots, holding the page layout, the card visuals, quality checks, and two PM weeks.

  • reachWorked out how to reach ad containers buried in site wrappers, and built a keyword list that gave the extension a fast, reliable way to find ad slots.
  • catchCaught dynamic ads with a MutationObserver and a polling fallback, which picked up slots that loaded after the first page scan.
  • sizeHeld each ad container at its original size during replacement, so the learning card fit the slot and the page layout never shifted.
  • designDesigned the card backgrounds, the fade-in, and the way each lesson reads inside the slot, and fixed transparency bugs that hurt text contrast.
  • verifyRan quality checks across several target sites, including pages owned by other team members, to confirm clean detection and replacement.
  • leadLed the team as product manager for two of the eight build weeks, covering the block-and-inject milestone and the onboarding and content-setup milestone.
  • testWrote the test cases for ad-blocker coexistence and non-English browser language.

The team

Built by five.

Harman Banwait Dave Jenks Diyahnara Lockhart Omar Schwartz Amanda Wartick

Faculty advisor: Professor Ankur Suri

Where it stands

What is true today.

Results

  • Works on nine of ten target sites.
  • Built in an eight-week capstone term.
  • Cards render in under two seconds from a local pool.
  • Passed a WCAG 2.1 A and AA audit of twelve issues.

Honest limits

  • Each user supplies their own Claude API key.
  • There is no offline fallback yet.
  • Detection leans on community filter lists.
  • Ad detection on the open web has no standard solution.

What comes next

Web Store, an offline library, more domains, ML detection, a retention study.
